prose:
**Fukui Reactivity Analysis of Ferrocene** — GFN2-xTB (xtb backend), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: input from `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz` (21 atoms, two cyclopentadienyl rings + Fe center), used as-is in single-point Fukui calculation. N+1 cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2) and N−1 anion (charge −1, multiplicity 2) states computed to derive f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, and dual descriptor per atom. Converged; all integrity checks passed.

**Most Nucleophilic Site:** Fe (iron center, 1-based atom 11), **f⁻ = 0.25938191** (highest propensity for electrophilic attack). Most electrophilic site: also Fe, **f⁺ = 0.22885456** (highest propensity for nucleophilic attack). The iron center dominates reactivity; negative dual descriptor (−0.030527) indicates electrophilic character. Hydrogen atoms show elevated f⁺/f⁻ (~0.063) vs. carbon (~0.014), reflecting acidic character. Charge conservation verified: Σ(f⁺) = 1.0000 and Σ(f⁻) = 1.0000, confirming convergence of both charged states. Mulliken partial charges (GFN2-xTB) show Fe at +0.420 (neutral), +0.680 (cation), +0.192 (anion). Bar chart visualization generated. Command: `chemkit fukui --method xtb --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz`. Result JSON: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpmlf8td52/live.json`. No warnings reported.
